title: Olive oil cake
subtitle: Italian-style almond and olive oil cake
excerpt: Olive oil cake uses a fruity olive oil instead of butter, ground almonds for body, and orange for flavour. The crumb is dense, moist, and stays good for several days. There is no buttercream or icing — a dusting of icing sugar is enough.
sourceNotes: Olive oil cake is a fixture of Italian and broader Mediterranean domestic baking, documented across regional Italian cookery. The combination of ground almonds, eggs, olive oil, and citrus is a classical Ligurian and Tuscan form, widely published in twentieth-century Italian domestic cookbooks translated for British audiences.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze sliced and wrapped. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: This cake improves with 24 hours' rest. Store covered at room temperature.

glossaryTerms.definition: Ground almonds (sold as almond flour or almond meal) contribute fat, moisture, and a soft, dense texture to cakes without adding gluten. They absorb liquid slowly, which means cakes made with a high proportion of ground almonds stay moist longer than flour-only cakes. The natural oils in the almonds also provide richness without the need for additional fat.

content.text: The base of this cake is mostly

rather than flour, which makes it naturally gluten-free and gives it the dense, moist quality that Italian olive oil cakes are known for. Use a fruity, mild olive oil (something you'd be happy to dip bread in), not a peppery cooking oil.

Heat the oven to 170°C (150°C fan). Grease a 23 cm round cake tin and line the base. Whisk {{eggs}} and {{caster-sugar}} together for 2 minutes until pale. Whisk in {{olive-oil}}, then add the {{orange}} zest and juice. Fold in {{almond-flour}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{salt-table}} until just combined. Pour into the tin and bake for 35–40 minutes until golden and a skewer comes out clean. The centre may dip slightly — that is normal for an almond cake. Cool in the tin for 15 minutes, then turn out onto a rack. Dust with {{icing-sugar}} when completely cool.
